turns.content: My SQL query is taking 45 seconds to run on a table with 2 million rows. SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_email = 'test@example.com'
I also have WHERE clauses on order_date and status frequently. Should I create separate indexes for each?
What about SELECT *? I've heard that's bad practice.

Correct — SELECT * has two problems. First, it retrieves all columns even if you only need two, increasing I/O and memory. Second, it cannot use index-only scans. If your index covers all columns you need, PostgreSQL can answer the query from the index alone — but SELECT * forces it to go back to the heap. Always name your columns explicitly. SELECT customer_email, order_date, status FROM orders WHERE customer_email = ? is faster, clearer, and more resilient to schema changes.
